title: Strict equality operators should be avoided with dissimilar types
description: This rule raises an issue when a strict equality operator is used to compare objects of different types. The strict equality operator in JavaScript is represented by three equal signs (===), the strict inequality with (!==). It is used to compare two values for equality, but with an important difference from the regular equality operator (==).
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Avoid strict equality between dissimilar types - consider type conversion or loose equality
